claims.claim: We present a deterministic, reproducible benchmark for full-PDF citation accuracy applied to a public 80-page literature-review document.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: On the CENF Numeracy Literature Overview and Review PDF, the benchmark extracted 400 in-text citation mentions (261 unique keys) and 231 bibliography entries (233 unique keys).
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Of the 400 mentions, 291 resolved to an extracted reference key, yielding a mention resolution rate of 72.75% and a unique-key resolution rate of 67.05%.

claims.claim: The benchmark executes in 0.04 seconds wall-clock time with 14.2 MiB peak RSS on commodity hardware.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Citation accuracy—the property that in-text references resolve correctly to bibliography entries—is a basic quality criterion for academic documents.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Systematic assessment of citation accuracy across an entire PDF document requires parsing both the body text and the References section, normalizing author-year keys, and matching mentions to entries.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: This paper reports on the construction and execution of a small, reproducible, deterministic benchmark for full-PDF citation accuracy.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The benchmark is applied to a single public document: the CENF Numeracy Literature Overview and Review, an 80-page PDF containing a literature review with an extensive bibliography.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: This yields an estimated 42 body pages and 18 reference pages.

claims.claim: Citation mention extraction.** In-text author-year mentions are extracted from all body pages, covering both parenthetical citations (e.g., "(Smith, 2020)") and narrative citations (e.g., "Smith (2020)").
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.
